steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes literary forms that "straddle the line between prose and poetry" — forms with no fixed boundaries or clear category.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'neutral': means impartial or without strong qualities. Forms that straddle prose and poetry are distinctively hybrid, not neutral. ✗
  • Choice B — 'customary': means conventional or usual. Davis describes forms that resist established categories — the opposite of customary. ✗
  • Choice C — 'amorphous': means without definite shape or form. Forms that borrow from fables, realist narratives, and straddle prose and poetry are by definition shapeless and uncategorizable. ✓
  • Choice D — 'dispersed': means scattered or spread out in space. This describes physical distribution, not a literary form's structure. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
